programme.title: Alaskan Bush People

programme.title: Mountain Monsters

programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Gator Boys

programme.title: Treehouse Masters

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the final of the quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. The Crossworders battle the Lapsed Pyschologists to be crowned series victors

programme.title: Murder on the Victorian Railway
programme.desc: Drama-documentary examining the death of banker Thomas Briggs in July 1864, which was the first murder to be committed on a British train. The film uses testimonies from court transcripts, memoirs, letters and newspaper reports to tell the story of the crime through those connected to it, including engine driver Alfred Ekin - who found the body - and Richard Tanner, the detective in charge of the investigation. It also explores the huge public outcry and debate it provoked. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the second of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two teams of students get a second opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Oxford Librarians
programme.desc: Three men from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford librarians to try and make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: The story of the Hollywood female film icon, told through insights and anecdotes of friends, directors and fellow actors, who spoke about her over the years on a selection of BBC programmes. With archive interviews featuring stars such as Jane Russell, Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, as well as directors like Billy Wilder and John Huston, and Marilyn's former husband Arthur Miller

programme.title: Some Like It Hot (1959)
programme.desc: After witnessing a Mob hit in Chicago, two male jazz musicians don drag and hide out with an all-girl band to evade the gangsters pursuing them. However, the pair struggle to disguise their attraction for the beautiful lead singer - while one of them catches the eye of a smitten millionaire. Billy Wilder's classic romantic comedy, starring Jack Lemmon, Tony Curtis and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Nobody's Perfect: The Making of Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: Stars Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, together with other members of the cast and crew, recall the making of Some Like It Hot. Featuring rare colour footage from behind the scenes, providing an intimate portrait of Billy Wilder's classic Hollywood comedy

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn (2011)
programme.desc: A film student gets a job as a production assistant on the set of movie The Prince and the Showgirl. He bears witness to the clash of conflicting personalities between leads Marilyn Monroe and Laurence Olivier, and becomes a confidant of the troubled, lonely Hollywood actress. Fact-based drama, starring Michelle Williams, Eddie Redmayne, Kenneth Branagh, Judi Dench and Emma Watson

programme.title: Jazz 625
programme.desc: Archive material from the show originally broadcast in the 1960s, with performances by Duke Ellington, Dave Brubeck, Dizzy Gillespie and the Julian 'Cannonball' Adderley Sextet. Plus, rarely seen sessions featuring the likes of Wes Montgomery, Marian McPartland, Chris Barber's Jazz Band and Willie 'The Lion' Smith

programme.title: The Prince, the Showgirl and Me
programme.desc: Colin Clark uses his diaries written during the making of 1956 romantic comedy The Prince and the Showgirl to chronicle the gradual breakdown of relations between the film's stars Laurence Olivier and Marilyn Monroe

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.sub-title: Cambridge Quiz Society v Oxford Librarians
programme.desc: Three men from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford librarians to try and make connections between items that initially do not appear to be linked. Presented by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his exploration of the artistic history of the northern European region in Sweden - home of IKEA - exploring the country's tradition of furniture design

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on November 19, 1999. Featuring Geri Halliwell, Travis, Ricky Martin, A1, Will Smith, Stereophonics, Prince and Robbie Williams

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on November 26, 1999. Featuring Robbie Williams, Whitney Houston, Glamma Kid, Blur, Cliff Richard, Texas and Wamdue Project

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 28, 1981. Featuring The Polecats, UB40, Hazel O'Connor, Coast to Coast, Adam & the Ants and Kim Carnes, plus a dance performance from Legs & Co

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: John Peel and Janice Long present this pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 29, 1986. Featuring Doctor and the Medics, Robert Palmer, The Real Thing, Tears for Fears, Pete Wylie, Spitting Image and Peter Gabriel

programme.title: Miles Davis: Birth of the Cool (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary profile of the jazz bandleader, horn player and innovator whose restless determination to break boundaries and live life on his own terms made him a star, but also made him difficult to live with. In music and in life, Davis shied away from the status quo, and when he thought his work came to represent a new convention, he changed it again. Featuring studio out-takes, archive footage and photos and Davis's own words, as well as contributions from many friends and colleagues

programme.title: Alan Yentob Remembers - Ella Fitzgerald
programme.desc: Ahead of the opportunity to watch the great Ella Fitzgerald singing in 1974 at Ronnie Scott's nightclub, Alan Yentob sets the scene by sharing the fascinating story of how the broadcast came about. A tale of tenacity combined with new camera technology, Alan recounts how as young TV producer he managed to persuade Ella's manager to let the BBC film her in full flight at London's home of jazz, capturing forever what is now considered to be a performance of true musical significance

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald's Other Show
programme.desc: American singer Ella Fitzgerald in performance at Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club in 1974, originally broadcast as an episode of the arts documentary Omnibus in May of the same year. With Tommy Flanagan on piano, Bobby Durham on guitar and Keeter Betts playing double-bass

programme.title: Cleo Laine at the BBC
programme.desc: A look back at the singer's finest moments on the BBC, shown to coincide with her 95th birthday. Britain's 'First Lady of Jazz' became a star after joining The John Dankworth Seven in the 1950s, later marrying the bandleader. Internationally renowned for her mastery of the scat singing style, Cleo has now enjoyed over six decades of success and acclaim here and across the world. Featuring renditions of Send in the Clowns and I Love You Porgy, and duets with John Williams, Dudley Moore, Tony Bennett and her late husband Johnny Dankworth

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daddy dresses up in a spaceman suit and goes on the rampage in the toy department of a local shop, forcing an embarrassed Hyacinth to intervene. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy gets out of his depth when helping arrange Frank's wedding. The night before the event, he meets bridesmaid Liz

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high stakes celebrity quiz. Rugby player Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johannson must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for the charity of their choice with each correct answer

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Was Part of the World's Largest Scam
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Louise Minchin and Julia Somerville unite the victim of an international investment scam network with one of the investigative journalists who uncovered it. At least 32,000 people lost more than £200 million in the crime. Plus, how purchasing gift cards on someone else's behalf can leave someone vulnerable to being scammed, and the Liverpool fan who was left empty-handed when he tried to buy tickets from an unofficial seller

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui Joseph offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine presents from Naseby Antiques Fair, Northamptonshire, where experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks offer up their expertise to assist two teams as they search for profitable items to take to auction. Roo also brings along a mysterious antique to quiz the locals on what they think it is

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie is halfway through her college course and growing in confidence. She meets up with her EastEnders colleague Kitty Castledine, who talks about life using a wheelchair since childhood, giving Natalie a clearer sense of the realities and barriers that come with accessibility. On placement, she enters the home of Lisa, who has multiple sclerosis, and her partner Gary, who cares for her

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by Invictus Games gold medal winner JJ Chalmers. The pair head to Killicrankie, where they zip wire across the River Garry, before heading to their digs for the night. They also take a sledge ride through countryside and then head to the Blackruthven Estate, where they are given a drystone-walling lesson

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps members of a young family who have outgrown their London flat and now need a new home in rural Suffolk with enough room for two growing children. The exciting property line-up includes a mystery house that has six centuries of stories to tell - some written on its walls. Briony is also excited to meet a fellow baking enthusiast who, after making his own escape to the county a year ago, now sells cakes and runs a supper club from his home

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Diarmuid Gavin are in Croydon to help sisters Lynette and Natalie rescue their vast, sun-baked plot where weeds have taken over, vegetable beds are impossible to reach and a polytunnel and pallets leave little room for peace. The sisters dream of a peaceful retreat where they can reconnect with nature. Can the team transform this cluttered space for £5,500?

programme.desc: New Zealand comedy drama. The Oakley winery 'family' is shaken by a tremor and strained relationships. Daisy is stressed, Louis is leaving and Nic and Vic make a request. Starring Rebecca Gibney and Charles Edwards

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Daisy and Nic go for a walk, where they bump into the handsome Dr David. Gus and Griffin brave Morag's guesthouse for a romantic trip

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A woman wants a new lease of life in a rural home that has business potential. She's set her sights on either Devon or Dorset where presenter Briony May Williams has some options to show her. Briony also meets another entrepreneurial couple who've converted an old railway carriage into a fine dining destination.

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts the game show, which sees former England rugby star Ugo Monye, tennis player Judy Murray, goalkeeper David James and Scottish footballer Pat Nevin taking it in turns to cross the floor by only stepping on the correct answers - and doing their best to avoid the wrong ones. For the final crossing, those who have been successful join forces for a chance to take home the money for their chosen charities

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals reach their end, and four of the five remaining cooks will eventually make it through to Finals Week. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent have two demanding tasks up their sleeves, and will want to see glimpses of championship-winning food in all the plates delivered. The judges have invited pastry chef Philip Khoury, and the cooks are asked to recreate one of his signature desserts

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with journalist Jo Coburn and comedian John Tothill joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.desc: Winnie is ready to hit the road - but first needs to pass her driving test. Naturally, Agnes and the gang volunteer to help her practise, and chaos soon follows. With Winnie behind the wheel and Buster instructing, Finglas becomes a danger zone. Comedy, co-written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Danny O'Carroll and guest stars Shobu Kapoor and Marek Larwood

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Ron and Matt try to convince a child to have his vaccination. Alex helps Joyce with wedding planning, while Bruce and Serena butt heads over Matt. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, David Alan Grier, Mekki Leeper, Allison Tolman, Josh Lawson and Kahyun Kim

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Serena invites Alex to help out at her 'Botox and Bubbly' party, where guests get drunk and then enjoy a little anti-ageing treatment. Joyce enlists Matt's help with a personal issue and Bruce introduces Ron to the world of meme coins. Hospital comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Allison Tollman

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.desc: The best moments from the music event at Sunderland's Herrington Country Park, featuring headliners Fatboy Slim, Zara Larsson and Olivia Dean

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: In Turkey, Istanbul offers many distractions, and Mobeen struggles to keep the gang focused on their mission to rescue Aqsa and kill Khan. Comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen and the gang face Khan in a final showdown. Can Mobeen save Aqsa and his friends to get back home to the ends? Final episode of the comedy, starring Guz Khan

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: On a Bradford estate, Lenny and Mullet find an abandoned car and turn it into a DIY spaceship. Elif Riley, Logan Ludbrook, Lucy Francis and Hayley Brown star in this kitchen-sink sci-fi comedy about big dreams, everyday lives and escaping without going anywhere

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: David Attenborough introduces footage of animals in Asia that live at extremely close quarters with people, from tigers making a home in town to elephants stopping traffic for food. A small park in central Bangkok supports 300 giant lizards, whilst a flying squirrel in Taipei has made a cosy home in a school. Proboscis monkeys are forced to venture uncomfortably closer to humans, and swifts in Jerusalem face tough competition for nest sites

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. Arriving in Vietnam, they are challenged to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days. First they must buy suitable transport for the journey, and despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves more difficult than they expected, especially in one of the most expensive car markets in the world

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Africa, where they are challenged to buy a second-hand car costing less than £1,500 and in no way designed to go off-road, and drive across Botswana. Along the way they must negotiate salt pans, rivers and reserves full of animals while facing sweltering heat and suffocating dust

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part One
programme.desc: In the first of a two-part special edition of the motoring show, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Africa on a mission to find the definitive source of the Nile, the world's longest river, which spans more than 4,000 miles. The trio believe they can do a better job than explorers who claim to have discovered the waterway's starting point by travelling further and faster than previous efforts - but the only tools at their disposal are determination, ingenuity and three ageing estate cars

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Top Gear Africa Special - Part Two
programme.desc: After conquering traffic, dangerous mud slides and dubious map reading, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May conclude their quest to find the definitive source of the River Nile in Africa. The trio use a small raft to cross crocodile-infested waters and progress to Lake Victoria, where they hope to find a boat that will take them to the vast plains of the Serengeti. Then, after hundreds of punishing miles in their tired estate cars, the petrolheads make one final push to home in on their target

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Radio presenter Sian Eleri investigates the paranormal activity at Penyffordd Farm in north Wales, the house being widely considered the most haunted house in Britain in the late 1990s. She begins by meeting with specialist psychologist Dr Daniels who was called in by the family living on the farm at the time when they became desperate for answers for the strange activity in their home. The doctor explains that of the many apparently paranormal cases of his career, this was the one that stayed with him and still perplexes him to this day

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni investigates how Sean Combs has gone from being the world's biggest hip-hop mogul to haemorrhaging money, businesses, friends and fans in a matter of months. Yinka will examine how Combs made it to the top, and what some may have turned a blind eye to along the way. Speaking to some of Combs' inner circle, and biggest critics, Yinka will find out what they think about him and ask why so many are only speaking out now

programme.title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott visits Moscow, Idaho to uncover the story of the social media frenzy that followed the murders of four Idaho University students. She meets with online true crime sleuths building their own theories about the murders and those who were personally affected by the social media storm as she reflects on her own role in the tragedy and learns more about obsessive online consumption of true crime

programme.title: The Fast and the Farmer-ish
programme.desc: The teams compete in the semi-final, where they must escape from a vault, display an artistic side and succeed in a super-sized tractor driving challenge. One team thinks it's all over before they even start, but there are twists ahead, with places in the final at stake

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: 24 Hours with Ella Toone
programme.desc: Documentary offering unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for one of the most important days of her life - her wedding - while still coming to terms with the loss of her biggest fan, her dad. She opens up, alongside her fiancé Joe Bunney, on the impact of losing her dad, how his absence will be felt on their big day and talks through the decision to ask her uncle to walk her down the aisle. A double Euros winner with the Lionesses, Toone has built her life around football. For Ella and Joe, maintaining a life outside of this is a challenge, never mind planning a wedding alongside an already hectic sporting calendar

programme.title: Shrek (2001)
programme.desc: Anti-social ogre Shrek resolves to stop an evil ruler from banishing fairy-tale characters to his swampland home. However, the reluctant hero ends up forced to rescue the beautiful Princess Fiona from a castle guarded by a dragon, aided only by a wisecracking donkey. Animated comedy, with the voices of Mike Myers, John Lithgow, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy and Vincent Cassel

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Though the team members are looking forward to welcoming a new recruit into their midst, Mervin, in contrast, cannot wait to get away from the island. However, before he catches his flight, he becomes embroiled in the murder of a young man whose body has been recovered from a ravine. The case becomes more compelling when it is discovered the victim has left behind a mysterious message. Drama, starring Don Gilet and Don Warrington

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie has been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Emma Myers returns as teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi. Pip is determined to stay away from any more investigations and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings' trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case. Adapted by Holly Jackson from her novel Good Girl Bad Blood, and co-starring Henry Ashton, Zain Iqbal and Jude Morgan-Collie

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Connor becomes increasingly anxious about his brother's disappearance and begs Pip to help him find Jamie. Despite her reluctance, she eventually realises she has no choice and is adamant Max must be involved somehow. But when she seemingly hits a dead end, she is forced to go back to the drawing board and retrace Jamie's last steps

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie has been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Sian Eleri meets the current owner of Penyffordd, who bought the farmhouse despite the stories of its haunting. Dr Daniels suggests that moving the gravestone on the property is what started the paranormal activity, leading Sian to investigate if there really is a girl buried in the garden

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Sian Eleri's investigation into the reported haunting of Penyffordd Farm continues as multiple witnesses claim to see a hooded figure. Could they all have imagined the same thing? As she listens to recordings the original investigator made, Sian discovers the Welsh words for disabled and child had allegedly appeared on the walls. Sian meets with one of the Gower daughters to ask whether at the time she believed that to be the monk apparition focusing in one family member

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestone
programme.desc: Unable to meet Rose-Mary or David, Sian Eleri visits an exorcist to find out what he believes removing a spirit from the house would entail. She speaks to the daughters of the couple that owned the house at the height of the haunting to question them one last time and Dr Daniels finds a similar case in Spain that might provide a clue as to how the haunting might have been a hoax

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his 'glorious comeback'

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's time for the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000 - but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie has been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week continues, and the second set of seven amateur cooks must prove themselves enough to make it into their first professional kitchen. First they must prove to judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent they have what it takes by serving up a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook, with only the first best getting the chance to deliver a lunchtime service at the Straits Kitchen restaurant at London's Pan Pacific Hotel

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week begins with the first seven of the 14 remaining cooks given the chance to work in their first professional kitchen. But first, Anna Haugh and Grace Dent ask them to deliver a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook. For two, this will be the end of the road, while the others spend the day at the renowned Roe restaurant in Canary Wharf

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: OK Computer, How Do I Feel?
programme.desc: Hannah Fry explores whether artificial intelligence read emotions better than a human, as well as the new technology creeping into people's lives

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Derry/Londonderry

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is overcome by the amount of beige on show in a Telford flat. Dion Dublin heads downwards to reveal an unexpected bonus in Leeds. In Suffolk, there are character features for Jacqui Joseph to uncover

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Exploring the capital city by tram, Michael Portillo travels to the open-air museum of Memento Park in the suburbs. He discovers how a collection of imposing Soviet-era statues removed from the city following the fall of communism were placed here as a reminder of Hungary's past. In the north east neighbourhood of Rákospalota, Michael visits Origo Studio and learns how it's attracting big name Hollywood filmmakers

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Part one of two. The historian examines the turning points that led to America's declaration of independence from Britain in 1776 and asks whether this explosive split could have been avoided. The eight-year war that followed claimed thousands of lives and ended in a humiliating defeat for George III, and Lucy sets out to uncover why America chose this path and how Britain's missteps fractured an empire and shook the monarchy

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair. They answer question on the specialist subjects of Tudor rebellions, Christina Aguilera, the US Space Shuttle programme, and James Parkinson, before being tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: Oakley Wines enjoys new-found fame and welcomes a surprise visitor, while gala preparations take an explosive turn, putting the entire showcase at risk. Drama, starring Rebecca Gibney and Charles Edwards

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: Calf Breeze faces her biggest test yet as the dry season approaches, while teenage brother Buster seems intent on getting both of them into trouble. Iain Douglas-Hamilton sends daughter Saba on a mission to help one of the reserve's best-known elephants, Mountain Bull, who has been on a collision course with farmers and local villagers. Sarah Parish narrates

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel introduces more treasures from a visit to Harrogate, North Yorkshire. Featuring a table made from an ancient bedpost, a manuscript diary written by a woman who toured Great Britain in 1815, and an interesting example of scrimshaw

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Grimsby Minster in Lincolnshire, where Anita Manning and Michael Baggott assess maritime memorabilia including a ship's bell and a diver's knife. Paul Martin meets a Grimsby trawlerman on the very vessel he took to sea

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges ahead of Friday's final

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his travels in Budapest, Michael Portillo begins in the small town of Huvösvölgy, where he encounters a surprising railway run almost entirely by children. Back in the city, he visits the former home of Béla Bartók, one of Hungary's most famous pianists and composers. Michael then heads across the Danube to the headquarters of the low-cost airline Wizz Air

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Richard Osman and Ingrid Oliver search for profitable antiques in Norfolk, joined by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram. Raj and Ingrid have high hopes for some expensive West German pottery, but Richard and Natasha are discombobulated when their budget buy of a Scandinavian glass ashtray turns out to be a case of mistaken identity

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: The pressure builds as chicks prepare to fledge and predators close in. The team hope to capture even more intimate moments from the lives of the wildlife calling National Trust Crom home through their 24-hour remote camera surveillance. Away from Crom, Iolo Williams spends his final night live at Knepp, where rewilding is transforming the landscape

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Return of the programme that explores the lives of notable people, beginning with a look at Russian president Vladimir Putin's rise to power through 10 defining photos, examining how he has used imagery, TV and the media to control the narrative and consolidate his power. The film charts Putin's progress from an unremarkable childhood in Leningrad (now St Petersburg) to KGB agent in Dresden in East Germany, where the fall of the Berlin Wall and the collapse of the Soviet Union two years later changed everything

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl visits an amateur MMA fighter on probation whose sporting dreams have been put on ice after a historic conviction for assault against a former partner has resurfaced. Carl also meets a man who is getting his forklift licence - before he can even drive a car - and finds out how support from probation, and his peers, is helping him build a new life

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Katie Razzall

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Acting on a tip-off, the Peelers storm a city centre flat on a drugs bust. Inside, a suspect is startled, cuffed and arrested. Outside, the PSNI team search the nearby streets for the suspected 'big-time' dealer they think may have fled the scene with more drugs. In Belfast city centre, police attempt to arrest a man who refuses to comply with a search and the situation quickly escalates

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.sub-title: Blakeney Point - Bass and Sea Trout
programme.desc: Bob, Paul and Ted travel to Blakeney Point, a nature reserve and important nesting ground for terns and gulls, for a sea-fishing adventure off the north Norfolk coast. On the first day, the rain is lashing down, but they bravely meet the elements head on and fish in some of the most challenging conditions they have ever faced. After a day of mixed fortunes, they take a boat back to the car and head to their accommodation for the evening. Bear's Cottage was built in 1856 and is surrounded by 200 acres of trees, including giant redwoods and noble firs

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: As knockout week comes to a close, the remaining 10 chefs are tasked with making the perfect pizza, but with only four ovens between them they have some negotiating to do. Guest judge James Elliot, co-founder of Pizza Pilgrims, must choose the best four to send them straight to the semi-final. The last six chefs are challenged to make a dish inspired by their favourite takeaway

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers travel to Leeds, where they must transform studio apartments, creating cleverly divided open-plan spaces to entice potential renters in Leeds. Alan follows their progress throughout, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by former winner Lynsey Ford to critique the final results, before another hopeful is asked to pack up their design kit

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Response officers Anna and Perri race to the aid of a man who has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has a left a pedestrian badly injured

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Drama, starring Jessica Raine. Zoe and her family arrive on a remote Greek island, hoping a holiday reunion with friends will be an escape from reality. But her husband Dan's dark secret threatens to ruin everything, while a hedonistic first night party sets off a chain of events that will change them all forever. Zoe begins to wonder if there are bigger forces at play

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui Joseph offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his travels in Budapest, Michael Portillo begins in the small town of Huvösvölgy, where he encounters a surprising railway run almost entirely by children. Back in the city, he visits the former home of Béla Bartók, one of Hungary's most famous pianists and composers. Michael then heads across the Danube to the headquarters of the low-cost airline Wizz Air

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.sub-title: The Duke, His Wife and Their Lover
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain's great houses - and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provide support and trained assistance dogs to those living with deafness or hearing loss

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, who answer questions on the specialist subjects of the British Industrial Revolution, the films of Christopher Guest, Charles Rennie Mackintosh, and Succession, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Yorkshire by hot-air balloon. She meets two garden designers at Scampston Hall in Malton and catches up with an old friend at York Gate Garden in Leeds. She also learns about the Yorkshireman who became the father of aviation

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: The rains arrive in Kenya following the drought and elephant calf Breeze faces a fight for survival in the harsh conditions. Hundreds of elephants then descend on the reserve for the breeding season, and the research team prepares for the busiest period of the year. Using new technology, Saba Douglas-Hamilton tracks the bulls, but keeps a wary eye on rogue male Rommel

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Chichester Cathedral, West Sussex, where book specialist Clive Farahar examines an impressive autograph album and silver expert Alastair Dickenson finds a fine cow creamer and a rare freedom box. A pair of inlaid pistols also cause a stir of excitement, but an Oriental chest proves to be a disappointment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan make a final push for quizzing victory before the winner picks up that trophy

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Before boarding his train at Budapest's historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth. Heading south, his next destination is the Hungarian of Szeged, close to the Serbian and Romanian borders

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The search for Northern Ireland's greatest garden is almost at an end - five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting the five incredible gardens, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmount's walled garden in Co Antrim. The pair reveal which one of the five will be named the winner

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Some more unusual growing methods are demonstrated at Beechgrove Garden. Ruth Vichos and Brian Cunningham try producing a mushroom crop from a kit, and there's a report on some of Scotland's best bonsai, with everything to know about starting that form or cultivation

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is bringing bold colour to the garden, planting gingers and cannas, setting out tomatoes and sowing wallflowers straight into the soil. From her Devon garden, Carol Klein explores what is really going on inside a seed from the moment it is sown. Rekha Mistry is also on a mission to grow big in a small space, using only pots on her patio

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From a windswept island chapel battered by the North Sea to a quiet Welsh farmhouse that shaped the future of a language, this episode travels to two remote National Trust sites whose powerful stories have inspired centuries of pilgrims - and reveals the delicate conservation work protecting their legacy. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul fish for rudd at Thornwood Springs Fishery in Essex, a complex of three lakes on the edges of Epping Forest where they have to beware of the geese guarding the water. Bob then reveals that they will be camping overnight in St Nicholas's Church in the village of Berden, before Bob heads into Epping to drop off a bag of clothes at a charity shop

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Lucy Hockings

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Love & Mercy (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of the Beach Boys' Brian Wilson, following the singer-songwriter at two periods in his life. In the 1960s, he is at his creative peak, but haunted by inner demons that take their toll on his mental health. By the 1980s, he is a broken man dominated by a shady doctor, until a new relationship offers him a chance at recovery. Starring John Cusack, Paul Dano, Paul Giamatti and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Everything Went Fine (2021)
programme.desc: When André, 85, has a stroke, his daughter Emmanuèle hurries to her father's bedside. Sick and half-paralysed in his hospital bed, he asks her to help him end his life. Adaptation of Emmanuèle Bernheim's memoir, starring Sophie Marceau and André Dussollier. In French

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the West Midlands town of Dudley

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push her over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: The team performs new scientific tests on the ceramic material found in the Mesa, they uncover evidence that something top secret may be buried on Skinwalker Ranch

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Using audio recordings, official transcripts and incident reports, experts examine rumoured encounters between law enforcement and UFOs

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Hook Island Monster, Skydivers' Plane Stall, and Somalia Freighter Phantom
programme.desc: The team explores possible proof of a sea monster and a tale of a skydive gone wrong. Plus, a spectral image recorded on a Freighter ship in Somalia

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid hit the road in search and automotive junk to sell, starting at the HJ Pugh auction

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les loses his temper when the kids suggest he should sell their failing second store, while Seth decides to put a sneaky plan into action

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth's plan to sell the Pontiac shop hits a snag and Ashley is pulled into the fray, and a meeting with a potential buyer leaves them facing a life-changing situation

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les struggles to forgive Seth for trying to sell his business and decides to lock himself in the office. Meanwhile, a fight breaks out on the shop floor

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Stockport in the south of Greater Manchester plays host to David Dickinson and his team of dealers. David Hakeney spots a bizarre antique lamp, Simon Schneider forecasts a battle over a barometer and Brenda Haller examines a 1920s football programme

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: From Ilkeston in Derbyshire, where a lady in her nineties charms antiques expert Tony Geering with her 1920s motorbike helmet. Plus, Alison Chapman examines a wartime snuffbox, and auctioneer Annabel Brammer impresses David Dickinson and the team with her inside knowledge of rare local pottery

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The gang sizes up some signed presidential memoirs and a toy of Robbie the robot from the movie Forbidden Planet - but questions the value of a can of fake elephant manure

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The team examines one of the rarest guns in American history, and a tyre that once belonged to Nascar driver Dale Earnhardt

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues a set of glass lampshades, an oak sideboard and a pair of printers' trays heading for the tip and invites a couple of friends to bring them back to life

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid are granted access to the back of the Cotswolds Motor Museum

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Things get hairy in Henderson when an impromptu brawl breaks out among some of the bidders

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Jenny makes a series of extravagant bids to come out on top at an auction in Longview

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Satellites orbiting the planet more eyes on Earth's vast, unexplored areas of land and sea - revealing baffling new anomalies

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: How the falls of Ancient Egypt, Greece and Rome left the whereabouts of many of their greatest riches unknown. The hunt begins in Staffordshire, where the onset of a violent storm announced the discovery of the largest Anglo-Saxon hoard ever uncovered. Some felt the storm was an omen of things to come

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The team is offered antique photographs of outlaw Jesse James, and an amplifier for a 1950s organ

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick struggles to maintain his tough negotiating skills when offered a 1974 Volkswagen, and the team examines a Second World War fighter plane

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Vengeance: Murder on the Heath
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part fact-based crime drama. Best friends Gagandip and Ravi fell in love with student doctor Mundill, a love triangle that destroyed their friendship and ultimately ended in what the headlines called the honeytrap murder. Gagandip attempted to sexually assault Mundill, who conspired with Ravi on a revenge plot that went horribly wrong. Starring Dee Ahluwalia, Ikky Kabir and Sasha Desouza-Willock

programme.title: Vengeance: Murder on the Heath
programme.desc: Part two of two. At trial, Mundill was characterised as manipulative and ruthless. Mundill, Ravi and Ravi's accomplice Darren Peters were all charged with murder but ended up with different convictions and wildly different sentences. Drawing on police interviews, text messages, court documents and original interviews, this factual drama presents a complex picture of crime and punishment

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. The owner opened the establishment in 2001 with the help of her father but the business has failed to meet her expectations, and the chef realises that her ruthless style of management is having an adverse influence, not only on her staff but also on her family

programme.title: Flamin' Hot (2023)
programme.desc: In the mid-1970s, Richard Montanez works as a cleaner in California for Frito Lay, the snack giant responsible for many popular brands. Montanez suggests a spicy twist to one of the company's most popular lines, giving birth to Flamin' Hot Cheetos, which becomes a pop culture phenomenon. Director Eva Longoria's fact-based comedy drama, starring Jesse Garcia and Annie Gonzalez

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers a solutions-based guide to make brilliantly easy and delicious meals all cooked in one pan, pot or dish. Jamie cooks a traybaked salmon in a bag, tomato fritters that can be prepared in a frying pan, and roasted rolled pork with a risotto that are both cooked in the same casserole pan

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Susie and Bill soldier on with their barn conversion, but feel the pressure to make it liveable quickly so they can move in

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Amber Gill joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.desc: Rebecca thinks that she is in with a chance with Evan Drake when he invites her and Sam for a weekend aboard his yacht. Comedy, starring Ted Danson, Kirstie Alley and Tom Skerritt

programme.sub-title: To All the Girls I've Loved Before

programme.desc: Norm repaints Evan Drake's house while he is away, and gives Rebecca the chance to come over and look at his bedroom, but things go awry when Drake returns home early

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wonders how Ray would cope if she met an untimely end, and picks an attractive friend to replace her as his new wife - but cannot help being hurt by his enthusiastic response to the idea

programme.desc: A message mistakenly left on an answering machine leads to a possible romance for Frasier, who rushes off to the airport to meet the caller. Guest starring Linda Hamilton, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.desc: A cancelled date leaves Niles, Martin and Daphne suspecting Frasier's supermodel escort is just a figment of his fevered imagination - so he tries to provide photographic evidence of the relationship. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier go gift-shopping with a vengeance as Martin's 65th birthday approaches, vying with each other to be the apple of their father's eye on his big day. Starring John Mahoney, Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Niles throws a fancy-dress Halloween party with a literary theme as a benefit for the Library Association, which he attends dressed as Cyrano de Bergerac, while a misunderstanding lets one or two skeletons out of the closet. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4's midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Cumbria, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit collector Paula, who badly needs some cash to fix her leaking roof. Henry picks a Morris Minor for restoration, while Simon turns some unwanted wood into furniture that seriously impresses independent valuer Ronnie Archer-Morgan

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: The owners of a village in France officially open after two years of renovations. Cameras catch up with the man who rescued an abandoned hamlet in Spain who could now lose half of what he bought. Meanwhile, in Portugal, a couple start an animal sanctuary in their village

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Amber Gill in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul from south London has a lifelong dream to own a holiday home in the sun, where he can escape the winter and spend quality time with family and friends. Now that he's finally ready to take the leap, he's asked Ben Hillman to help him find the perfect property on Spain's southern Costa Blanca. He's also brought along his best friend Coral to help him with the search. Ben has five great options for Paul's budget of £80,000 - will any of them hit the mark?

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Liverpool man Lea reveals how 22 years ago he left a career in IT to move to Pasadena to run the Horse and Jockey pub and restaurant. Meanwhile, golf professional Peter is living the good life, earning much more money and working fewer hours doing a job he loves in the sunshine, and 74-year-old former estate agent Lynne from London is now enjoying life in The Villages - the largest retirement community in the world

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Montespieu, Louisa and Roger continue work on their Gingko Suite, but a shocking bathroom discovery could change everything. Debbie and Dave's craft room is derailed, while Donna and Paul are pushed to their limits after struggling to haul a standalone roll-top bath up three floors

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, young-at-heart clients Damien and Georgina are ready to build a new life in Spain. They come armed with bags of enthusiasm, a can-do attitude - but a very low budget. On the Costa Blanca, a more sizable sum is available to hunt out the perfect family villa for West Sussex couple Chris and Nicola and their three sons

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: George helps people across the country make the most of their space and design innovative homes. In Bromley, south London, George is helped by garden designer Luke Millard to come up with a stunning extension to include a gorgeous kitchen featuring curved arches for seasoned renovators Mitch and Elliot. An inspirational garden design is also part of their ambitious dream, but even for the most experienced home renovators, the best-laid plans can go awry as a flood scuppers their plans

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne sets another bunch of challenges for the celebrities, as a result of which Joanna Page does something startling with a pineapple, Armando Iannucci does something confusing with a nursery rhyme and Amy Gledhill does something unexpected with balls of ice. As if that wasn't enough, Kumail Nanjiani reveals his missing body part. Greg Davies dishes out the points

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: Comedy starring Sam Campbell. A hotshot director scours the country for everyday people with feature film ideas before racing against the clock to turn the idea into reality in just three days. Sam and his elite team set about bringing Mick Hall's incomprehensible concept - featuring a detective and real snakes - to the big screen. But it turns out that some people don't make good actors, and snakes have a tendency to bite

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: The residents of Smiling Spring Care Home want to make a sci-fi epic about a group of retirees who go inside a computer to defeat online scammers. But the shoot goes sideways when Pat makes moves on a wealthy resident, Winnie gets challenged to a fight and Jess discovers that she's truly terrible with old people

programme.title: Dante's Peak (1997)
programme.desc: A vulcanologist's suspicions that a long-dormant volcano is about to erupt turn out to be true, prompting him to race against time to save the community standing in its way - and clash with local government officials weighing his predictions against the loss in tourism revenue. Disaster movie, starring Pierce Brosnan, Linda Hamilton, Elizabeth Hoffman and Charles Hallahan

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Engineer Ellie and nuclear project manager Callum are both self-confessed nerds. But will their quiet chemistry prove the perfect solution? Author and dating expert Beckie is ready to leave toyboys behind, as she meets romantic poet Paul. DJ John wants to relight his love life. He's matched with high-energy environmentalist Susha. Seventy-two-year-old Andy hopes to strike the right balance with footballer Jan

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: The story of a man whose partner convinced him she had terminal cancer, and that she had a miscarriage. Devastated by the news, he doubled down on supporting her and goes to work renovating the home of her friend. What he discovers at that house unravels the many layers of deception that she has been weaving

programme.title: Second Act (2018)
programme.desc: A lack of a college degree costs a woman with an abundance of street smarts a promotion in her job despite her success. Her godson responds by creating a fake profile for her that details a Harvard education and world travel, but there is a big surprise in store for her in her new role at a corporate firm. Romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Milo Ventimiglia and Vanessa Hudgens

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three rival couples battle it out to host the best dinner party in Newcastle. Partners Fiona and Hannah kick things off with their Moroccan-themed menu. Couple David and Sonia, and childhood sweethearts Claire and Steven, all agree the puppet show was unexpected. Day two sees Steven and Claire's German-inspired menu. Finally, David decides a charm offensive is needed for his and Sonia's night

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: James and Jodie are pushing to get one room habitable in their dilapidated old farmhouse, while Dave learns how to keep sheep that are going to be shown in competition

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Amber Gill in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier helps Carla overcome her terror of flying, while Rebecca anxiously awaits a visit from a restaurant critic. Bar-room comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: The Sam in the Grey Flannel Suit
programme.desc: Rebecca is astounded to learn Sam has been promoted to an administrative position in the company that owns Cheers - until she realises they only want him to play on the corporate softball team. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody decides he deserves a pay rise, but is fobbed off with a new, important-sounding job title. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Robert asks if he can borrow the suitcase sitting on the stairway landing, Ray explains he and Debra have been locked in a two-week battle of wills over who is responsible for putting the item away. Ray Romano and Brad Garrett star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Marie shame Ray into giving Robert a second bachelor party to make up for his disappointing first attempt

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Robert and Amy prepare to tie the knot in a classic Barone family wedding ceremony - but the groom becomes suspicious when his future brother-in-law Peter tries to make peace with both him and Ray. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Brad Garrett

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roz panics at the thought of becoming a mother, prompting Frasier to lend her a friendly shoulder to cry on - while he ponders the father's identity. Award-winning comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Special episode filmed on location in Seattle. Frasier is guest of honour at a public rally to mark his 1,000th radio show - but the celebrations go awry as Niles's jealousy reaches new peaks. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is asked to be a guest speaker on an all-star cruise, and invites the family to accompany him. However, the so-called celebrities also appearing are not exactly famous. Meanwhile, Niles's marital problems worsen once he discovers Maris will also be coming along

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio psychiatrist begins dating a successful lawyer, but it is not long before conflicting career demands start to get in the way of their relationship

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay tries to help a struggling seafood restaurant in Manhattan, where the three owners regularly come into conflict as they each try to assert their authority over the business, causing confusion for all. When one of the trio stubbornly resists the changes the Scottish chef is hoping to implement, sparks begin to fly

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4's midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Suffolk, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet former agony aunt Janet, whose garden shed is crammed with goodies. With no motors to unearth, Henry makes a rare furniture discovery, while Simon finds a vintage tram bench

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: A couple from Norwich get married at their hamlet in the Limousin. Meanwhile, in the Dordogne region, two estate agents reveal plans to renovate a stunning chateau. And in Portugal, a couple from Eastbourne try to pick themselves up after a wild fire rips through their village

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Amber Gill in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps an engaged couple from Shropshire to find a new holiday home in the seaside town of Gran Alacant on Spain's southern Costa Blanca before their wedding

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Joanne ticks off some outstanding jobs on a property she is renovating to sell as a side hustle. Lea juggles running his busy British pub with organising a surprise birthday party for his wife, and Andreas's daughter is in town for the summer holidays

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Jane and Rory dive into a new project and Anna and Maxime tame a wild, overgrown courtyard. Louisa and Rodger face the challenge of finishing a new suite before a wedding party arrives - knowing if they fail, they'll have a very unhappy bride on their hands

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Emotions run high in Almería, as Sharon and Steve Garner take on the task of finding Isle of Man couple Stephen and Andrew a new permanent home under the Spanish sun. Meanwhile, Birmingham childhood sweethearts Kevin and Liz are hoping to relocate to the Costa Blanca after 40 years together and are putting their trust in husband-and-wife team Jo and Andrew Alderton

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Matt Shea sets out on the trail of wanted criminals evading justice in an attempt to reveal the shocking scale of this country's hidden fugitive crisis. His journey leads him from safehouses in London to an underground network in Thailand, as he uncovers a shocking new mentality among criminals, where justice is optional. What does this mean for victims of crime and for confidence in the criminal justice system?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Costumier Scott usually hides behind the drag queen Casta Hex, but tonight he's shedding his alter ego as he sits down with George, who may strut in like a catwalk star, but hasn't dated in five years. Former Olympian Simon and 60-year-old stable manager Louise are both seeking love later in life, while disco queen Priscilla and bhangra-loving accountant Raj both want fun, but can their musical tastes mix? Twenty-three-year-old Jack is feeling nervous about his blind date and hopes he can find some common ground with Rachella

programme.title: Central Intelligence (2016)
programme.desc: Action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson as a one-time geek who suffered constant humiliation at high school but grew up to be a lethal CIA agent. But when he is targeted by his own organisation, he comes home for a high school reunion, where he enlists the help of the former campus tough guy - now a meek accountant - who very quickly wonders what he has got himself into. Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Alex Horne sets another bunch of challenges for the celebrities, as a result of which Joanna Page does something startling with a pineapple, Armando Iannucci does something confusing with a nursery rhyme and Amy Gledhill does something unexpected with balls of ice. As if that wasn't enough, Kumail Nanjiani reveals his missing body part. Greg Davies dishes out the points

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Newbury Park in southern California to help Mexican restaurant Mama Rita's. The owner previously ran a successful catering company but has struggled to adapt her skills to meet the demands of a new business - and with a year's worth of food stored in the freezer Gordon realises he has a tough job ahead

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Swallows Rest in Weymouth, Dorset, where hosts Jane and Keith Smith are pulling out all the stops to win. However, the colour combinations in the rooms do not go down well with rival B&B owner Ray Chowdhury, and an afternoon of drawing starts some competition between Ray and newlyweds James and Darren Kosa-Jones

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the Burley Inn in the New Forest, where newlyweds Darren and James welcome their guests. Donna and David prove to have a critical eye when they discover holes in the sheets, and Jane and Keith have a disagreement over a toilet roll. In the afternoon, the couple reveal their quizmaster skills, before Ray grills them on how they run their B&B over dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Hotel Montville in Redditch, Worcestershire for the third day of the competition, where first impressions of Ray Chowdhury's unique style and decor are positive. However, room inspections leave Jane and Keith Smith threatening not to stay, and the guests grill their host about his business background at dinner. Although some light relief is given in the form of ice sculpting, breakfast disappoints James and Darren Kosa-Jones, and Ray must face the harsh reality of his guests' opinions at feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to Donna and David's Silverton B&B in Shrewsbury, where the complimentary earplugs soon have Darren and James worrying about the noise from the road outside. A Segway race helps whip up the guests' competitive spirit, Donna gets emotional when her work-life balance is questioned, and chef David proves he can handle the unexpected at breakfast

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Engineer Ellie and nuclear project manager Callum are both self-confessed nerds. But will their quiet chemistry prove the perfect solution? Author and dating expert Beckie is ready to leave toyboys behind, as she meets romantic poet Paul. DJ John wants to relight his love life. He's matched with high-energy environmentalist Susha. Seventy-two-year-old Andy hopes to strike the right balance with footballer Jan

programme.title: Vengeance: Murder on the Heath
programme.desc: Part two of two. At trial, Mundill was characterised as manipulative and ruthless. Mundill, Ravi and Ravi's accomplice Darren Peters were all charged with murder but ended up with different convictions and wildly different sentences. Drawing on police interviews, text messages, court documents and original interviews, this factual drama presents a complex picture of crime and punishment

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Boca Raton in Florida to turn around the fortunes of Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's. The owner opened the establishment in 2001 with the help of her father but the business has failed to meet her expectations, and the chef realises that her ruthless style of management is having an adverse influence, not only on her staff but also on her family

programme.title: Flamin' Hot (2023)
programme.desc: In the mid-1970s, Richard Montanez works as a cleaner in California for Frito Lay, the snack giant responsible for many popular brands. Montanez suggests a spicy twist to one of the company's most popular lines, giving birth to Flamin' Hot Cheetos, which becomes a pop culture phenomenon. Director Eva Longoria's fact-based comedy drama, starring Jesse Garcia and Annie Gonzalez

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers a solutions-based guide to make brilliantly easy and delicious meals all cooked in one pan, pot or dish. Jamie cooks a traybaked salmon in a bag, tomato fritters that can be prepared in a frying pan, and roasted rolled pork with a risotto that are both cooked in the same casserole pan

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Susie and Bill soldier on with their barn conversion, but feel the pressure to make it liveable quickly so they can move in

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Amber Gill joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Rebecca thinks that she is in with a chance with Evan Drake when he invites her and Sam for a weekend aboard his yacht. Comedy, starring Ted Danson, Kirstie Alley and Tom Skerritt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: To All the Girls I've Loved Before
programme.desc: The gang throws Frasier and Lilith stag and hen parties, but after hearing grim stories about marriage, the groom begins to question his decision to walk down the aisle

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm repaints Evan Drake's house while he is away, and gives Rebecca the chance to come over and look at his bedroom, but things go awry when Drake returns home early

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is too ill to take Ally to a pyjama party, so Ray reluctantly agrees to do the honours. Unfortunately it means coming face to face with Peggy, an old enemy who physically assaulted him during a charity event

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra wonders how Ray would cope if she met an untimely end, and picks an attractive friend to replace her as his new wife - but cannot help being hurt by his enthusiastic response to the idea
