programme.title: Elvis Presley: The Searcher
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about the singer, beginning with his early life in Tupelo, Mississippi, and his unprecedented rise to fame over a single year

programme.title: Elvis Presley: The Searcher
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary about the singer, beginning with his return home after his discharge from the army, and how he dealt with a rapidly changing pop scene

programme.title: I Am Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A celebration of the country singer-songwriter built around 12 of his classic tracks spanning four decades, and featuring interviews with his family and friends. Featuring contributions from Sheryl Crow, Kris Kristofferson, Willie Nelson, John Mellencamp and Kid Rock

programme.title: Searching for Sugar Man (2012)
programme.desc: Documentary following two fans in search of their musical idol. American singer Sixto Rodriguez enjoyed a brief recording career in the 1970s before fading into obscurity. However, his politically conscious music became incredibly popular in South Africa. Bizarre stories of his death had circulated over the years, but two devotees plan to find out what really became of him

programme.title: Wirecard: A Billion Euro Lie
programme.desc: Documentary lifting the lid on the scandal that rocked the financial sector across the globe - the rise and billion-dollar fall of financial payment firm Wirecard

programme.title: When We Were Kings (1996)
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors offering personal opinions on its importance to them

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the Italian actress, one of the highest profile European stars of the 1950s and early '60s

programme.title: UFO Hunters
programme.desc: The Hudson Valley area of upstate New York, where more than 7,000 UFO sightings have been reported. This edition speculates whether the area is home to one of the many extra-terrestrial gateways that are believed to be spread all over the world

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette head to north Wales to investigate waterways that may contain signs of the medieval Welsh Rebellion. Conwy Castle was constructed by Edward I between 1283-1287 after the Welsh Rebellion of 1282-3. The castle was part of a chain of fortifications known as the 'Iron Ring of Castles' and was both a material and symbolic sign of English power and dominance. Edward I selected its site on a high rock perched above the estuary of the River Conwy so that the castle could be supplied by the river in times of siege. Day two finds the pair at Ruthin Castle, a medieval fortress overlooking the River Clwyd

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A Kazakh farmer discovers ancient burial sites that may determine whether or not the legend of the Amazon women warriors is myth or reality. In the Italian Alps, a group of hikers explores long forgotten military infrastructure that points to the region's complicated and violent history. A shrine in the Indian Himalayas has long been said to be protected by supernatural powers, but strange markings on the temple reveal it was subject to an almost equally as powerful force. Palaeontologists discover a fossilised species unknown to science embedded in a rock hundreds of millions of years old

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: The pickers hit the city of Niagara Falls, Ontario, on their hunt for big deals. Their first stop finds them in the middle of the strip staring down a collector's memorabilia and bizarre pieces. They later head to view items at the city's oldest restaurant

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The push for private commercial space flight and the launch of Donald Trump's Space Force. The programme includes a discussion of the scientific effort to look for traces of Martian life, the idea that Earth life originated from Martian life that travelled via a space rock and a look at the challenges likely to be faced if attempts are made to colonise the moon and Mars

programme.title: History of Weapons
programme.desc: A team of experts tests the deadliest weapons upgrades across the history of warfare, including the Scorpio, the long sword and Henry's rifle

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, as captured soldiers, sailors and airmen on both sides dared the impossible to win their freedom. This episode focuses on the German submarines that caused havoc in the American desert, the playboy pilot they couldn't cage and the young lovers whose escape defied all odds

programme.title: Bomber: Terror of WWII
programme.desc: How the bombing of Tokyo demonstrated the power of the B-29 bomber. The cost of creating this machine exceeded that of the Manhattan Project, which developed the first atomic bomb

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The push for private commercial space flight and the launch of Donald Trump's Space Force. The programme includes a discussion of the scientific effort to look for traces of Martian life, the idea that Earth life originated from Martian life that travelled via a space rock and a look at the challenges likely to be faced if attempts are made to colonise the moon and Mars

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A young boy makes headlines when he uses satellite images to discover lost cities of the Maya. Trees in China seem to spell out a sinister message

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The myth of Merlin, which associated with an arrangement of stones in northern France. Satellite images are called upon to finally explain this ancient riddle

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try to get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena. An image of a wrecked aeroplane triggers a hunt that leads to a Colombian drug lord, a series of mounds in Romania provide possible evidence of a vampire burial site, while a mysterious ancient city is spotted on a coral reef

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images. After 70 years, images help identify the hiding place of the multi-billion dollar Nazi Gold Train, while a blood-red river in Siberia points to pollution on a catastrophic scale. Plus, satellite evidence indicates the Vikings may have travelled further into North America than anyone previously imagined

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A structure shaped like a giant shark provides likely evidence of a Bahamian lost civilisation. Plus, the satellite images of hundreds of thousands of strange oval scars on the North American landscape leaving experts baffled and analysis of an image of a massive hurricane that shows it has thousands of animals trapped in its eye

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Korean at D-Day, Stealth Chopper, Arctic Nazi Base, The Caspian Sea Monster
programme.desc: The story of a Korean soldier allegedly captured during D-Day, who purportedly fought for the Nazis, raising questions about his true allegiance and the nature of his captivity. Plus, the mysteries surrounding an Arctic Nazi base and the possible existence of a Caspian sea monster

programme.title: Greatest Escapes of WWII
programme.desc: A look at some of the most incredible escapes of the conflict, as captured soldiers, sailors and airmen on both sides dared the impossible to win their freedom. This episode focuses on Hitler's last soldier in America who defied the FBI for 40 years, the prisoners who revealed the secrets of the death camps and the merciless Nazi who almost got away

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes with Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Audacious jail and prison breaks, with insight from Hayes, whose legendary escape from a Turkish prison was the basis for the Oscar-winning movie Midnight Express. Each episode includes interviews with the survivors, witnesses, historians, and law enforcement of each event

programme.title: The Secret Nazi Expeditions
programme.sub-title: The Quest for the Holy Grail
programme.desc: Various traditions describe the Holy Grail as a cup, dish, or stone with miraculous powers capbale of providing eternal youth or sustenance in infinite abundance. As the Third Reich rose to power, Himmler and the SS became fixated on the Grail, interpreting much of the myth surrounding it as fact - and the quest for it as attainable

programme.title: Hitler's Engineers
programme.desc: Hans Kammler's fingerprints can be found all over the Third Reich's most prestigious and deadly engineering projects, including rockets and concentration camps

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The push for private commercial space flight and the launch of Donald Trump's Space Force. programme.title: UFO Hunters

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette look for evidence of Roman life in Scotland, heading to Ardoch Fort in Perthshire, on the east bank of the River Knaik. It spans over eight and a half acres and was built under Agricola around 80AD on the east bank of the River Knaik. The fort occupied a key strategic route through Perthshire and into north-east Scotland from the First to Third centuries

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. They join a team comprised of archaeologists and the military attempting to uncover the role this building played in the war effort. They also visit Britain's oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the German's could not kill

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A secret military base discovered a few hundred miles from the North Pole. A man-made, ancient stone formation is found 120ft below the surface of Lake Huron. A 1,500-year-old burial ground is discovered on the shores of the Baltic Sea. Plus, in Russia's far east, a life-form that scientists think can live for thousands of years is discovered in the permafrost

programme.title: Canadian Pickers
programme.desc: While exploring the Ottawa Valley, the pickers come across an old English manor filled with five generations of valuable antiques

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Erich von Daniken's Chariots of the Gods? suggests that aliens once visited Earth. Fifty years after the book's publication, experts contemplate whether he is he about to be proven right and that extraterrestrials with a superior knowledge of science and engineering did indeed land on Earth thousands of years ago

programme.title: History of Weapons
programme.desc: Experts take a look at three weapons that have been used to attack civilian populations, including the brutal trebuchet catapult and, possibly the most terrifying of all, the atomic bomb

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The story of one of the world's most effective war machines ­ the Viking longship. Its design evolved from swift war canoe to versatile and feared ship, changing the course of history

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The vessels that operate in secret, unseen by their enemies, from secret missions that helped win the Second World War to modern stealth ships using strange shapes and technology to avoid spying ears and eyes

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: How bad design, human error and the enemy sent great ships to tragic ends, such as Britain's K13 submarine and HMS Hood, Sweden's 17th-century Vasa, and Kublai Khan's fleet, sunk by the original kamikaze

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The ships that made mighty empires, from the lone wolves that patrolled the high seas for imperial Britain to the USS Laffey's heroic Second World War battle against kamikazes at Okinawa

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Stories of espionage at sea, including deadly U-boat cat-and-mouse games during the Great War, the sinking of the Lusitania, a gun-smuggling private yacht that made history and liners-turned-troopships

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Project Iceworm, Mystery of Sea Lab, Soviet Night Witches, Weirdest Tank Ever
programme.desc: Veterans Rudy Reyes and Ronnie Adkins break down the evidence behind the most mysterious photos, films, and recordings in battlefield history. This episode considers what dangerous materials are held in an abandoned American Cold War military base in the Northern Arctic and whether a saboteur really did shut down the US Navy's classified Sea Lab

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes with Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Billy focuses his attentions on the notorious Texas 7, a group of prisoners who escaped from the John B Connally Unit near Kenedy, Texas, in December 2000. They employed strength, wit, disguises and plenty of teamwork to achieve the feat

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes with Billy Hayes

programme.title: UFO Hunters

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Two-part documentary exploring the Anglo-Norman invasion of Ireland. The first edition opens in the turmoil of 12th-century Ireland, where provincial kings compete for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henry's vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: The second of the two-part documentary follows the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171 Henry II arrived to takes control of Ireland, and he granted land there to settlers from Britain. Now archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives. The episode also shows how the conquest of Ireland enabled centuries of war between England and France

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and a team of experts visit a 15th-century Welsh manor house, believed to be haunted by many of its previous owners

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team investigates the Clockhouse in Surrey, where medium Derek Acorah senses strange goings-on and picks up the name of Ruth. A conversation with the property's owner, Fred Batt, reveals that the mysterious woman could be a 1950s hostess who is said to haunt his London nightclub, Caesar's

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: Robin and the Hoods (2024)
programme.desc: For the tenacious 11-year-old Robin and her loyal band of friends, the Hoods, the patch of overgrown scrubland at the end of their cul-de-sac is a magical kingdom. However, when the slippery property developer Clipboard dazzles the parents of the area with promises of health spas and day-care facilities, the gang risk losing their kingdom forever. Director Phil Hawkins' comedy, starring Naomie Harris, Darcey Ewart, Gwendoline Christie and Mark Williams

programme.title: Rebellious (2024)
programme.desc: A princess is kidnapped by an evil sorcerer and her scholarly fiance must use his wits in an epic quest to find her, while she boldly challenges her captors and plots a daring escape. Animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Vanessa Johansson and Marc Thompson

programme.title: Pil's Adventures (2021)
programme.desc: Pil, a young orphan living on the streets of Roc-en-Brume with her three tame weasels, disguises herself and embarks on a quest to save the heir to the throne and victim of an enchantment. Animated adventure, with the voice of Eleanor Noble

programme.title: Jack the Giant Slayer (2013)
programme.desc: A farm boy accidentally rebuilds the link between the human world and a kingdom of man-eating giants in the sky, resulting in a princess being trapped in the monsters' realm. He joins a band of the king's knights on a rescue mission, but one of the group intends to betray them in a bid to seize the throne. Fantasy adventure, starring Nicholas Hoult and Ewan McGregor

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: Hook (1991)
programme.desc: Peter Pan, who has finally grown up and is now a married lawyer with a family of his own, is whisked away by Tinkerbell to Neverland, where he must learn to fly again if he is to save his children from the clutches of Captain Hook. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
programme.desc: An unassuming hobbit is plunged into a battle against the forces of darkness when his uncle bequeaths him a magical ring of immense power. He joins a fellowship of heroes to embark on a perilous quest to destroy the ring before it falls into the hands of its creator - a dark lord who needs it to regain his power and conquer the world. The first part of Peter Jackson's epic fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkien's novel, starring Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen, Viggo Mortensen, Sean Astin, Christopher Lee, Liv Tyler and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (2002)
programme.desc: Frodo and Sam continue their quest to destroy the all-powerful ring, bringing them face to face with the magical artefact's twisted former owner Gollum. Meanwhile, their allies prepare to make a desperate last stand against the vast armies of the corrupted wizard Saruman, who has declared war on a neighbouring kingdom. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Sean Astin, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Billy Boyd, Dominic Monaghan, Andy Serkis and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
programme.desc: As hobbits Frodo and Sam near the end of their journey into the dark lands of Mordor to destroy the One Ring, the evil Sauron unleashes the full might of his armies on Middle-earth. Meanwhile, Aragorn must fulfil his destiny of becoming king by leading his people into a climactic battle with the forces of darkness. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen and Sean Astin

programme.title: Jack the Giant Slayer (2013)
programme.desc: A farm boy accidentally rebuilds the link between the human world and a kingdom of man-eating giants in the sky, resulting in a princess being trapped in the monsters' realm. He joins a band of the king's knights on a rescue mission, but one of the group intends to betray them in a bid to seize the throne. Fantasy adventure, starring Nicholas Hoult and Ewan McGregor

programme.title: Hook (1991)
programme.desc: Peter Pan, who has finally grown up and is now a married lawyer with a family of his own, is whisked away by Tinkerbell to Neverland, where he must learn to fly again if he is to save his children from the clutches of Captain Hook. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith

programme.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017)
programme.desc: A mysterious map sends Smurfette, Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty on a quest into the Forbidden Forest, leading to the discovery of the biggest secret in Smurf history. Animated adventure, with the voices of Demi Lovato, Julia Roberts, Rainn Wilson and Joe Manganiello

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (2002)
programme.desc: Frodo and Sam continue their quest to destroy the all-powerful ring, bringing them face to face with the magical artefact's twisted former owner Gollum. Meanwhile, their allies prepare to make a desperate last stand against the vast armies of the corrupted wizard Saruman, who has declared war on a neighbouring kingdom. Part two of Peter Jackson's fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Sean Astin, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Billy Boyd, Dominic Monaghan, Andy Serkis and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
programme.desc: As hobbits Frodo and Sam near the end of their journey into the dark lands of Mordor to destroy the One Ring, the evil Sauron unleashes the full might of his armies on Middle-earth. Meanwhile, Aragorn must fulfil his destiny of becoming king by leading his people into a climactic battle with the forces of darkness. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen and Sean Astin

programme.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017)
programme.desc: A mysterious map sends Smurfette, Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty on a quest into the Forbidden Forest, leading to the discovery of the biggest secret in Smurf history. Animated adventure, with the voices of Demi Lovato, Julia Roberts, Rainn Wilson and Joe Manganiello

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan

programme.title: Journey 2: The Mysterious Island (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager picks up a distress signal originating from the middle of the ocean. Joining an expedition to track the signal to its source, he discovers an unexplored island inhabited by strange creatures, and searches for a way to rescue its sole human inhabitant. Fantasy adventure sequel to Journey to the Centre of the Earth, starring Dwayne Johnson, Michael Caine and Josh Hutcherson

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Ende's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: Robin and the Hoods (2024)
programme.desc: For the tenacious 11-year-old Robin and her loyal band of friends, the Hoods, the patch of overgrown scrubland at the end of their cul-de-sac is a magical kingdom. However, when the slippery property developer Clipboard dazzles the parents of the area with promises of health spas and day-care facilities, the gang risk losing their kingdom forever. Director Phil Hawkins' comedy, starring Naomie Harris, Darcey Ewart, Gwendoline Christie and Mark Williams

programme.title: The Last Airbender (2010)
programme.desc: A chosen one with mastery of the four elements is charged with keeping the peace between four nations. When he disappears while still a boy, one of these factions sees a chance to gain absolute power and wages war on the others. After 100 years, the chosen one is found and must find a way to restore harmony. Fantasy adventure based on the cartoon series, starring Noah Ringer and Dev Patel.

programme.title: Journey 2: The Mysterious Island (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager picks up a distress signal originating from the middle of the ocean. Joining an expedition to track the signal to its source, he discovers an unexplored island inhabited by strange creatures, and searches for a way to rescue its sole human inhabitant. Fantasy adventure sequel to Journey to the Centre of the Earth, starring Dwayne Johnson, Michael Caine and Josh Hutcherson

This documentary follows the players, managers and supporters of various teams as they strive to win the 2025 tournament and have their name inscribed in Gaeltacht folklore. programme.desc: Meemaw is determined to catch George Sr in a lie, Sheldon encourages Missy to question the Bible, and Mary helps Pastor Jeff search for a youth pastor

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Potential Energy and Hooch on a Park Bench
programme.desc: Georgie upsets the family when he makes a life-changing decision, while Sheldon tries to convince Dr Sturgis to return to the university

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Howard plan respective birthday celebrations for Amy and Halley. A letter from his brother makes Leonard feel anxious about his own lack of accomplishments. The series takes a mid-season break until the new year.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy subject their friends to a series of secret tests to find out who is most qualified to act as best man and maid of honour at their wedding. Penny realises her true feelings about her friendship with Amy

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Sheldon kicks Amy out of the apartment so that he can have some 'alone time' to work, she and Leonard bond during a series of science experiments. Bert the geologist replaces Howard in his band Footprints on the Moon, and Sheldon finds Penny to be a surprising source of scientific inspiration. Laurie Metcalf and Brian Posehn guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj finds himself in the middle of a domestic drama when he realises that the woman he's dating, Nell, has a husband, Oliver, who is very upset about their new relationship. Sheldon rents his old room back to have a quiet place to work and drives Leonard crazy by being a model tenant

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon petitions Wil Wheaton to allow him to appear on the new Professor Proton show, so the actor offers a guest appearance to Amy instead. Meanwhile, Penny gets angry when she thinks the female protagonist in Leonard's novel is based on her

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette's friends each try different tactics to help her go into labour. Howard isn't happy when he finds out that Bernadette has already chosen their son's name without his input

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While Leonard jumps through hoops to help secure the perfect wedding venue for Sheldon and Amy, Howard and Bernadette have a difficult time deciding which one of them should stay at home with the kids and who should return to work

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny gets the opportunity to spend time with Bill Gates at work, because his business wants to partner with her pharmaceutical company. Leonard, Howard and Raj do everything in their power to also meet the high-tech entrepreneur, while Sheldon thinks he's the victim of an April Fool's Day prank

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Valentine's Day sees Molly play Cupid. After hearing Harry's tragic break-up story, she attempts to pair him with her sister Victoria. Meanwhile, Carl and Samuel try to pick up single women at the Laundromat. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike decides to take things into his own hands in his quest to find out if he is fertile enough to get Molly pregnant. Meanwhile, Vince starts working as a door-to-door vacuum cleaner salesman. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike storms off over a disagreement with Molly, so she decides to go on a shopping spree at the mall. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Pish Posh and a Secret Back Room
programme.desc: Meemaw opens a gambling room, while Sheldon, Missy and Georgie adapt to new living arrangements. Comedy, starring Iain Armitage and Annie Potts

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Stuffed Animals and a Sweet Southern Syzgy
programme.desc: Georgie has a solution for Meemaw's business woes, Sheldon and Dr Linkletter struggle to solve an equation and George Sr gives Missy advice on her love life

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Money Laundering and a Cascade of Hormones
programme.desc: Missy asks some uncomfortable questions at church, while Meemaw and Georgie go into business together. Comedy, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard runs against Sheldon to be president of the tenants' association, while Bernadette encourages Howard and Raj to track down the owner of a drone they find in the back garden

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: A brilliant but reclusive scientist, Dr Wolcott, invites Sheldon to his cabin in the middle of nowhere, so Leonard, Raj and Howard go along for the trip. Penny and Bernadette improvise when Amy doesn't like the tame bachelorette party they have planned for her. Peter MacNicol guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon isn't pleased by the newfound popularity of Stuart's comic book shop when an author puts the place on the map. When Raj attempts to take the credit for Penny's astronomical discovery, friendships are tested. Howard and Bernadette try to arrange a date night

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon goes to Las Vegas and does everything he can to win a large sum of money for science. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette take Amy shopping for a wedding dress, but her terrible choice entangles them in a web of lies

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Sheldon learns that his mother won't attend his wedding unless he invites his brother Georgie, he and Leonard travel to Texas to end a family feud. Meanwhile, Howard and Bernadette's children inadvertently give them, Amy and Raj conjunctivitis. Jerry O'Connell guest stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Friends and family gather as Amy and Sheldon prepare to tie the knot - but it seems the happy couple's minds are elsewhere. Jim Parsons and Mayim Bialik star, with guest appearances from Laurie Metcalf, Kathy Bates, Mark Hamill, Jerry O'Connell and Teller (the quiet half of US magic duo Penn and Teller)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's honeymoon runs aground in New York, Penny and Leonard discover that they are uncomfortably similar to Amy's parents, while Raj insults astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson and starts a war on social media. Kathy Bates and Teller (of Penn and Teller fame) guest star as Amy's parents

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy drive themselves to distraction trying to figure out what the 'perfect gift' is that Leonard and Penny have got them for their wedding. Meanwhile, Raj decides he wants to settle down and asks his father to arrange a marriage for him

programme.title: 90 Day Fiance: Before the 90 Days
programme.desc: Part one of two. The couples share the latest updates on their relationships. A new confession cam captures unfiltered opinions, and tensions are at an all-time high.

programme.title: 90 Day: The Single Life
programme.sub-title: The Act of Making it Official
programme.desc: Gino files for divorce from Jasmine and finds new love. Kim and Wayne decide their relationship status. Cortney kicks out Colt. Pedro ghosts Sophie.

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper: Breaking Out
programme.desc: Jason isolates due to a giant neck growth. Marisa dreads spider veins after surgery and Kobe hums to stay calm as a huge bump near his eye is removed.

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper: Breaking Out
programme.desc: Dr. Lee and Zoe treat Mary Ann's crusty skin. Isaiah ignores a lump on his spine and Maressa fears treating the oozing skin covering her ankles.

programme.title: My 600lb Life
programme.desc: At 700 pounds, 35-year-old Margaret is virtually bedbound. It's time for her to overcome her food addiction so she can finally live her life

programme.title: Curvy Brides Boutique
programme.desc: Jo and Al show bride Ciara the perfect dress does not have to be way over budget, and after a mastectomy, bride Joanna has an ingenious way to get her curves back

programme.title: Curvy Brides Boutique
programme.desc: Levi has lost three dress sizes since she bought her 50s-style wedding dress, so Jo and Al try to find her one that fits. Plus, Caroline is losing sleep over her gown

programme.title: Curvy Brides Boutique
programme.desc: Terrified bride Kirsty longs for a confidence-boosting gown. Meanwhile, tomboy bride Laura never wears dresses, so Jo and Al try to convince her to embrace her feminine side

programme.title: Permissive (1970)
programme.desc: A woman comes to London to visit an old friend, and gets drawn into the world of rock band groupies. She quickly sinks into a self-destructive hedonistic lifestyle, and is consumed with jealousy as she and rival groupies bitterly compete to outshine each other in the eyes of the musicians they cling to. Drama, starring Maggie Stride and Gay Singleton

programme.title: Musketeer
programme.desc: An old musketeer meets a youth and two scoundrels on the road to Paris. Short drama, starring Kevin McNally, Andrew Lewis and Charlie Allen

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke are creating their latest tipple from tasty foraged seaside ingredients. In Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp, while newbie hoteliers Steph and Luke prepare for the busiest weekend of the summer season. Down the coast in Filey, military vehicle restorer Duncan preps his Second World War American Jeep for parade duty

programme.title: Highclere: The Real Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The team prepares to host their first charity gala in three years in the castle's iconic library. The event involves a clay pigeon shooting competition, followed by a three-course lunch and a big-ticket auction, but staff shortages put them under pressure to deliver the top-notch service that will encourage the guests to dig deep

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Alan and Val McCoy put their life savings on the line to make their dream of living on water come true, entrusting a boat builder to make them their dream narrow boat floating home. The couple have been together for 11 years and now that they're retired, they've decided that owning a narrow boat could be a unique way to be near their families - all of whom live along the canal system across the Midlands and north-west England

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill arrive in Bolton, Greater Manchester, where they set about transforming Debbie's tired and overgrown plot into a relaxing Southeast Asian-inspired haven, a perfect spot to remember her late father and practise yoga. For her £3,000 budget, Debbie would like to share her garden with as much wildlife as possible and would also love a natural pond

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill redesign Iqbal and Pardeep's empty garden in Oldbury, Worcestershire, to reflect their love of sport and the outdoors while also providing a space for their teenage children to spend time with their friends or for family parties

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea

programme.title: The Greatest Auction

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave is in Gosport knocking down an internal wall to turn a property's outside toilet into a cloakroom

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Reclaimed bricks are used during a masterclass in old-English brickwork from the ground up

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: This edition charts the exploits of Pat and Sue Willan, who now retired plan to buy their very last floating home, which they hope to keep moored on the Thames. They have enlisted the help of a boat-maker in Stoke and opted for a 35-tonne, 55-foot state-of-the-art, all-mod-cons Dutch barge. They have a healthy budget of over £400,000 and elaborate plans for very contemporary kitchen fittings and gadgets and a powerful hydraulic engine

programme.title: Highclere: The Real Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house holds a celebration of the centenary of the exhibition that discovered the tomb of Tutankhamen, led by the great-grandfather of the current Lord Carnarvon. Lady Carnarvon is writing a book about her husband's famous ancestor, while housekeepers Sheena and Jason are cleaning the Egyptian museum in the basement. Once the domain of the maids and footmen, it now houses replicas from the tomb of Tutankhamen and is a treasure trove that requires them to use special cleaning techniques

programme.title: Yorkshire by the Sea
programme.desc: Fisherman Darren heads into the North Sea from Staithes in search of lobster and crab. Shellfish are now a mainstay of the catch on the Yorkshire coast, with conservationists ensuring that the lobster population remains buoyant by breeding and releasing them from a purpose-built hatchery in Whitby. Plus, a new recruit joins the team running the historic tramway in Saltburn, and an ambitious couple launch the first wine grown in their Robin Hood's Bay vineyard

programme.title: New Home DIY

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: South African-born Linda, her husband Mark and Coco the cat share a one-bedroom floating home on Tagg's Island in Hampton, Surrey. It's an idyllic location, but their home is also their workplace and it's starting to feel a bit cramped. The couple want to stay put in their exclusive location, so Linda has hatched an ambitious plan to buy the boat next door and do it up all by herself. It's a huge challenge and it remains to be seen whether enthusiasm and passion will be enough to get the job done

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Uttoxeter, east Staffordshire, Shail and Cecilia long for a garden that reminds them of growing up in the countryside of India. However, the new-build garden they own is a featureless sloping plot. Lee Burkhill and Flo Headlam pool their efforts to create an entertainment space in the middle of the garden with a central patio featuring bespoke screesn and L-shaped benches. Lee adds a home-made tandoor for the family to cook together and a food prep area and vegetable growing beds. Flo mucks in with a planting area to draw attention from the giant walls and herb garden by the back door

programme.title: The World is Full of Married Men (1979)
programme.desc: Saucy, glossy romp adapted by Jackie Collins from her own novel _ and therefore a bit thin on plot, but bursting with heaving bosoms and heavy jewellery. It tells of an advertising executive's decidedly sordid extramarital escapades, and his neglected wife, who eventually gets tried of his philandering _ and decides to take her long-awaited revenge by giving him a well-deserved taste of his own medicine. Starring Anthony Franciosa, Gareth Hunt, Paul Nicholas, Carroll Baker and Georgina Hale.

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: A journalist is found dead at a martial arts contest, raising the possibility that two competitors are guilty of murder. The team calls the suspects in for an interview, but Jane comes up with an alternative plan to ensnare the culprit. Elsewhere, an internal investigator questions Rigsby about his father. Drama, starring Simon Baker and Robin Tunney

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: After Jesse's close friend vanishes, Charlie and Rex discover the man was living two separate lives

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: Saunders shoots a boy in self-defence, but the absence of a weapon suggests the victim was unarmed. When the deceased's father arrives at the precinct, taking hostages and creating a stand-off, the suspended officer gives himself over to the negotiator in a bid to defuse the situation, while Carrie relies on her remarkable memory to unlock the secret of the missing gun and clear her colleague's name

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A student backpacker is found dead in the showers of her hostel, and with a gun in her hand and her body slumped against the only door, all evidence points toward suicide. Humphrey isn't so sure, however, especially when he notices all her toiletries laid out and her towel missing. But when the team question the victim's boyfriend and her fellow backpackers, they find themselves with very little to go on. Meanwhile, JP prepares for the biggest day of his life as his wedding day approaches, and when Humphrey bumps into an old lady friend, Florence encourages her boss to pursue his quest for love

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Fall of the House of Newsome
programme.desc: Rupert Newsome is accused of murdering his wife Lucinda, and despite his choice to represent himself in court, Effie tries her best to help

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: A criminal profiler joins the team to help investigate a double murder case that bears resemblances to crimes committed a decade previously by a serial killer known as the Caveman. Jane clashes with the newcomer's scientific approach and disagrees with her theories about potential suspects - so he fabricates a piece of evidence to throw her off course in the hope of outwitting the real culprit. Crime drama, guest starring Linda Park (Raines)

programme.title: Hudson & Rex
programme.desc: When a magician dies during a practice-run of her latest routine, Charlie and Rex must look beyond the smoke and mirrors to uncover the truth

programme.title: Unforgettable
programme.desc: A conversation with Walter Morgan at a formal event suggests to Carrie he holds key information about several murders, including that of her sister. The death of a tennis player reveals a link between the lawyer and the previous sniper calls, and when Morgan develops an unhealthy interest in Carrie, she must decide whether to form a friendship with him to solve the mystery of her sister's death

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the 12th series of the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Jo Brand, Olivia Colman, Stacey Dooley, Aston Merrygold, Diane Morgan, Bob Mortimer, Richard Osman, Lucy Porter, Jon Richardson, Henning Wehn and Shaun Williamson to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory. Joining regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are snooker veteran Steve Davis, actor Craig Parkinson, comedian Sara Pascoe and Strictly co-host Claudia Winkleman. They all have amazing stories to share, but whether or not they are true or works of fiction remains to be seen

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Miles Jupp, Deirdre O'Kane and Phill Jupitus join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'non sequiturs' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers are on alert for a cartel ambush as they transfer a Sicario to the Mexican military. A random inspection of a pick-up truck reveals 32kg of narcotics

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Documentary with unrestricted access to America's Customs and Border Protection working on the bridges that separate Texas and Mexico, following the work of the many officers who police the bridges and keep America safe. In the first episode, a shipment of cardboard packaging hides a deadly secret, and heroin disguised as a laptop baffles the world's most advanced narcotic detection device

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team take on a salvage mission of titanic proportions in very dangerous territory as they fight to shift a concrete yacht that has been underwater for 12 years.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand, singer Emma Bunton, athlete Jonnie Peacock and actor Shaun Williamson join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire test their general knowledge skills in the hope of winning a prize

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team explores driving safety, and Jeremy Clarkson goes on manoeuvres in a Hummer - a military-style jeep - and Sweden's contribution to the world of supercars is revealed. Neil Morrissey is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May and Richard Hammond look at convertibles, comparing the VW Beetle, Citroen Pluriel, Mercedes CLK, Mazda MX5, Daihatsu Copen and Audi Cabriolet. Jeremy Clarkson puts the Nissan 350Z through its paces, and supermodel Jodie Kidd is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the impact of Britain's colonial past on the nation's food, making a bread and butter pudding with chai and adding Indian pale ale to a sausage casserole. They also investigate the origins of gin and tonic and a brand of Worcester sauce, both of which arose from the UK's imperial past

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.desc: Part two of two. While in Venice investigating his friend's death, Lovejoy discovers that not only is the beautiful Caterina saving art for herself rather than posterity, but so too is her sister Lavinia. Fulton Mackay, Gwen Humble and Haydn Gwynne guest star, with Ian McShane

programme.title: This Is Not a Murder Mystery
programme.desc: New guests arrive at West Dean, bringing tension and poor manners. As Quant and Thistlethwaite race to connect dots, a masked figure with a face of roses emerges

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Boulton and Stanton's night in a luxury hotel goes horribly wrong when a series of robberies attracts the unwelcome attentions of DS Beech

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Melody tries to help an elderly woman whose obsession with hoarding has led to her being threatened with eviction. Jimmi makes a surprise return to work, and Michelle is annoyed when Ruth and Mike look to be getting close on a night out. Medical drama, starring Elizabeth Bower and Adrian Lewis Morgan

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Heston tricks Michelle by arranging for a patient to feign the symptoms of meningitis. The practice nurse also takes time for a girlie chat with Ruth, during which she divulges she is having second thoughts about Mike, before the receptionist in turn reveals information that leaves her friend reeling

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Diane's anxiety over the coroner's inquest into Dominic's death is compounded by feelings of nausea, and Ric suggests her physical symptoms might mean she's pregnant. Matt and Dean compete for the attention of a patient's daughter, but a hung-over Mubbs has no time for their messing around. However, he's grateful for the help when they stop him making a serious mistake

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jeff and Dixie suspect an elderly man of using their ambulance as a taxi service, but Lloyd investigates the patient's medical history and uncovers the cause of his health complaints. A boy is seriously hurt after being caught up in a dispute between two warring families, and Lenny receives upsetting news. Starring Michael Obiora

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Boulton and Stanton's night in a luxury hotel goes horribly wrong when a series of robberies attracts the unwelcome attentions of DS Beech

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A guilty Ricky makes a decision he may live to regret when Sam gets in touch, while back at home an oblivious Bianca resolves to spice up her marriage

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As Sam hovers on the brink of going into labour, Ricky struggles to confess to Bianca that he may be the baby's father. Ian presents Jane with an offer she cannot refuse

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: When a despotic soap opera director has his throat cut in the middle of a scene, the producer wants Harry and Fergus to find the culprit so filming can resume

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Lucien investigates when one of Ballarat's most prominent surgeons is found dead on his own operating table, and ends up being drawn into a web of jealousy and sexual intrigue. Meanwhile, as Chief Superintendent Munro's enmity towards the doctor continues, an old ally returns from Melbourne. Australian detective drama, starring Craig McLachlan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom decides to go on hunger strike at the Bayview Retirement Village until the standard of food improves. First episode of the sitcom, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The friends meet a mysterious man who has a machine that can locate 'hidden forces'

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The trio's curiosity is aroused when they encounter a man sporting an inflatable doll who wants to show the world his admiration for fat people and obesity, and hostility towards slimming and weight loss. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley is almost ready to unveil his latest creation, but his odd behavour makes the trio want to find out what lies within his shed sooner than he would prefer. Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde, Bill Owen and Gordon Wharmby star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Kembleford Ramblers' annual outing takes a tragic turn when the local lord is found strangled, so the cleric gets his walking boots on to solve the crime. Murder mystery, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Art Detectives
programme.desc: DI Mick Palmer, sole member of the Heritage Crime Unit, is dispatched to the imposing Astleigh House in Yorkshire, where art historian Francis Crozier has been murdered

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired detective Steve McAndrew arrives from Glasgow to help reinvestigate one of his original cases when a missing girl's DNA is found at the scene of a petrol station robbery in Clapham, south London. It's clear that the inquiry is a very personal one for McAndrew, and although his personality rubs the team up the wrong way, his methods soon pay dividends. Denis Lawson stars

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective duo investigate a series of murders, all of which seem connected to the Wetherton Arts Centre where a production of Oliver! is being rehearsed. However, the case takes an unexpected turn when the latest victim is a little closer to home. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer is arrested on suspicion of armed robbery - and to make matters worse, the only way he can clear his name is by admitting he was with a charming ex-girlfriend at the time. He must decide whether to come clean and face a jealous Charlotte's wrath, or take the consequences of the charge. Ian McShane stars

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: While a visiting theatrical troupe puts on a nativity play at St. Judas, a precious holy relic goes missing, and the actor playing Jesus is killed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A softer side of Smith emerges after he helps deliver a baby. Alex Walkinshaw stars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi is relieved to find he has someone to confide in when Ronnie tells him he knows the truth about Eva. Mike makes an effort with Michelle, but the couple break up. Vivien asks for help preparing for her forthcoming court case, and George helps a man make a better job of caring for his cancer-stricken wife

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When a pretty student wakes to find herself in the class geek's bed, it sparks a day from hell which ends at the Campus Surgery

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Zubin gives evidence at the inquest into Dominic Fryer's death, where his personal conflicts with Ric are called into question and he is left reconsidering his future. However, on his return to the hospital he discovers a potentially lethal mishap with the oxygen supply and has to race against time to save a patient's life. News of Diane's pregnancy spreads across the wards, but she faces a difficult choice when Connie offers her a prestigious research position. Chrissie warns Owen the baby changes nothing - his relationship is still a terrible mistake

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dixie is forced to take a call during her break, only to become frustrated by what appears to be a hoax. Unhappy with her working conditions, she complains to ambulance controller Louise, but their feud has fatal consequences. Meanwhile, Dylan goes beyond the call of duty to help a frightened youngster whose mother appears to have taken an instant dislike to him

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A softer side of Smith emerges after he helps deliver a baby. Alex Walkinshaw stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A vengeful Janine wreaks havoc for Bianca and Ronnie. Peggy is left devastated by Sam's shocking announcement, while Billy enlists Jay in his hunt for Phil

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Becca reveals her true colours in a shocking showdown with Stacey. Peggy tracks Phil down and is horrified by what she discovers. Ryan has a romantic surprise for Janine

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: A mystery novelist is found dead in a locked panic room with no gun. Private eyes Fergus and Harry investigate the peculiar case while fending off the meddlesome Harry Benedict

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: A hotel owner finds a body in one of the rooms and Blake's investigation leads him to a woman in Mattie's care who has lost her memory after a car crash

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The staff at Bayview Retirement Village are sent into a panic when Diana and Tom head off to Brighton for the day in a borrowed Porshe without telling anyone. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo is stunned to catch Nora Batty in a church hall closet with Billy Chiselhurst. Realising he has a rival for her affections, he challenges Billy to a duel at dawn. Bill Owen, Kathy Staff and John Jardine star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler comes face to face with the woman of his dreams and promptly sweeps her off her feet - on to the back of his tandem, and Barry resolves to throw caution to the wind and show his affection for Glenda. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Jean Alexander, Thora Hird and Stephen Lewis alongside stalwarts Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy encounters an unlucky chimney sweep, and sets out to find him a good old-fashioned chimney to sweep intent on helping preserve the tradition. Comedy, starring Brain Wilde

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Lady Felicia's home comes under threat, Father Brown is forced to seek help from an unlikely ally. Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll star

programme.title: Happy Valley
programme.desc: Sgt Catherine Cawood discovers the remains of a gangland murder victim in a drained reservoir, sparking a chain of events that leads straight back to Tommy Lee Royce. Catherine's grandson Ryan is now 16 and has his own ideas about the kind of relationship he wants to have with the man Catherine refuses to acknowledge as the boy's father. Meanwhile, in another part of town a local pharmacist gets in over his head when a neighbour is arrested. Crime drama, starring Sarah Lancashire, Siobhan Finneran and James Norton

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The body of a missing computer expert turns up in a hospital morgue under a false name, so the team reopens the investigation into his disappearance. The dead man worked for the Metropolitan Police, giving him access to a great deal of sensitive information - added to which, he was in contact with a group of online hackers. But he died of natural causes - so why was his body hidden, and by who? As the seasoned detectives unravel the mystery, Brian struggles to accept Steve as the new Ucos member, forcing Esther to intervene. Guest starring Sarah Smart and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan as the chalk-and-cheese detectives. The mysterious death of a wealthy entrepreneur opens up a hornet's nest of family tensions, betrayal and cover-ups involving radioactive waste - and brings Dalziel face to face with an old flame. Cherie Lunghi guest stars

programme.desc: A penniless sculptor seeks the canny dealer's help to prise a considerable inheritance out of his avaricious brother. Celia Imrie guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: River Walks

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher walks an eight-mile route along the River Lea, taking in the landmarks of an industrial past and discovering what the future holds for the waterway in this fast-growing part of London

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his quest to trace the southern tropic, travelling from South Africa to Madagascar. In Zimbabwe, the presenter comes face to face with the harsh realities of the refugee crisis, and witnesses a unique method of land-mine disposal in Mozambique. Heading to the Madagascan islands, he discovers an unusual local delicacy, and visits a town where the poor make a living by digging for sapphires

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his journey, crossing the vast expanse of mainland Australia. Heading east from Ningaloo Reef, the explorer comes across a ghost town in Wittenoom Gorge, contaminated beyond habitation by a nearby asbestos mine. Travelling across the Northern Territory, Simon witnesses the shocking living conditions endured by Aboriginal communities in the shadow of Uluru

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey around the world, Simon Reeve follows the tropic across South America. In Argentina he meets an English anthropologist who married into a forest tribe. On the Paraguay-Brazil border he visits a hotbed of smuggling activity, and in Sao Paulo, the globetrotter takes in a neighbourhood formerly considered the most dangerous in the world

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville encourages Gastric to become a fearless hunter in an effort to impress Madge and get closer to her. Mavis senses Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, making a move on Granville and decides to stand up for herself for once. Mr Newbold goes to the library in the hope of meeting up with Mrs Rossi, while Eric and Cyril finally get their wives to go camping - but who is going to help with the washing-up? David Jason, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Maggie Ollerenshaw star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Tim and Kate's closeness following the death of his grandmother becomes a cause for concern for Lee, so he shares his worries with a counsellor - who is unimpressed by his lack of social skills. The subsequent funeral descends into chaos, thanks to an irate elderly relative and an addictive drink

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee pretends to be gay to find out more about a new business acquaintance - but only because his reward for doing so is tickets to Wembley. His deception is soon uncovered and he is taken to a 'specialist' club to test his alleged sexuality. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The new controller wreaks havoc at Maplins as he sets about firing numerous members of staff, and upsets Peggy in the process. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom and Toby disagree over the private and national health systems. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - children's birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him and he locks himself away in a cupboard, so it is up to Agnes, Father Quinn and best man Buster to try to calm him down. programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy's constant presence in the flat continues to rile Lee and and his distrust is fuelled further when he discovers his unwanted housemate runs a lap-dancing club. programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alison's discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The veteran broadcaster meets more inmates inside two of Indiana's most dangerous women's prisons. Among them are Dawn Hopkins, who was sentenced to life for murdering her three-month-old son, and Sarah 'Cindy' White, who was incarcerated as a teenager and will spend the rest of her life behind bars. The journalist also hears the remarkable story of Linda Darby who, after escaping from jail in 1972, spent the next 35 years on the run - before the law finally caught up with her

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A cosmopolitan, appearance-focused mum-of-three switches places with the mum from a down-to-earth farming family who has four children and hundreds of animals

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Four cooks vie for the chance to sit out Sunday's elimination. In the first round, contestants must produce a dish using the ingredients found in mentor Shannon Bennett's fridge

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets the Sweet family in Weston-super-Mare, whose three children have all been diagnosed with multiple rare illnesses and will soon be full-time wheelchair-users. It may also affect their speech, fine motor skills and vision. All three children already struggle with basic normal life at home, despite the love and care of their parents Cat and Chris. So the DIY SOS team - plus hundreds of local workers - are here to give them the four walls they need

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bangor in Co Down to help Mandy McCreight, who is isolated in her bedroom due to a variety of medical issues and is missing out on family life. Her children Ben and Kara also have mobility problems, while husband Davey looks after everyone, but now he is suffering from a back injury. The entire family are under real stress and the house is not wheelchair friendly, making the situation unbearable
